title: Jarell

'''Jarells''' were a [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] [[Reptile/Legends|reptiloid]] [[Species/Legends|species]] native to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Oon Tien]] in the [[Kathol sector/Legends|Kathol sector]]. They developed a primitive society on their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]], organized into matriarchal [[City/Legends|city]]-states, before contact with [[Colony ship/Legends|colony ships]] from the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] approximately three centuries prior to the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]].

Jarells were a historically violent species; the various [[King/Legends|monarchies]] that ruled Jarell society forced their own young into periods of indentured servitude in exchange for weapons and technology. Since their discovery by the Republic, Jarells migrated to several other planets in the Kathol [[Sector/Legends|sector]] and were often found serving private citizens, corporations, and criminal organizations as [[Mercenary/Legends|hired muscle]].

==Biology and appearance==
Jarells were a large [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]]<ref name="tdc" /> [[Reptile/Legends|reptiloid]]<ref name="CSWE" /> species who possessed great strength. Jarells had mottled skin, which was predominantly [[Color/Legends|purple]] and white, but included blue patches on the [[Limb/Legends|arms]] and forehead.<ref name="tdc" />

They had wide skulls with four bony<ref name="CSWE" /> cranial ridges that were present on their heads, starting at the brow and running over the scalp to the nape, from which short spikes protruded. Jarells possessed red [[Eye/Legends|eyes]]. Instead of a [[Mouth/Legends|mouth]], Jarells<ref name="tdc" /> had a mass of thick tendrils<ref name="CSWE" /> that covered the lower portion of their face. Several small bony growths sprouted on their elbows, and their fingers terminated in sharp claws.<ref name="tdc" />

==Society and culture==
Jarells were a society ruled by [[King/Legends|monarchies]], divided into various [[City/Legends|city]]-states across their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Oon Tien]]. A historically violent [[Species/Legends|species]], Jarells were considered to be dim-witted by other beings. However, Jarells were actually a creative and perceptive species, with excellent memories and a tradition of oral storytelling.<ref name="tdc" /> They spoke [[Jarellian]], a language that had several different dialects,<ref name="votf" /> but no written form.<ref name="tdc" />

[[File:Oon_Tien.jpg|thumb|left|Oon Tien, the Jarell homeworld, depicted on a map]]
Following contact with the outside [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]], many of the [[Planet/Legends|planet's]] monarchies made arrangements for young Jarells to serve periods of indentured servitude to corporations, private citizens, criminal organizations, and even [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Governor/Legends|governors]] as hired muscle and general servants. The length of service was usually between seven and eleven [[Standard year/Legends|years]], and while it was essentially [[Slavery/Legends|slavery]], young Jarells were socialized to accept it as a natural period of their lives. They saw it as a period of maturation, symbolizing their transition from child to adult.<ref name="tdc" />

While few Jarells actually ever profited from the arrangement, they hoped that their servitude would introduce them to beings who could make them powerful, or help them acquire wealth. As a result, young Jarells on the cusp of their service looked forward to the [[Time/Legends|time]] with excitement and purpose. While generally loyal to their employers, some Jarells were willing to undermine them if it would benefit them to do so.<ref name="tdc" />

==History==
Jarells originated on the planet Oon Tien in the [[Kathol sector/Legends|Kathol sector]]. The species lived in monarchic city-states and had a knowledge of basic metal-working when they were first contacted by [[Colony ship/Legends|colony ships]] from the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]<ref name="tdc" /> approximately three hundred years before the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]<ref name="CSWE" /> in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]].<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref>

The [[Human/Legends|Human]] settlers subsequently established several settlements on Oon Tien, and lived side-by-side with their Jarell neighbors. Although technologically primitive, the Jarell monarchies began trading gemstones and periods of indentured servitude from their young in exchange for weapons and other high-technology items with the Humans. The weapons were used by the monarchies in a series of armed conflicts and revolutions that changed Jarell society, although the fighting never spread to any of the Human settlements on the planet.<ref name="tdc" />

Following contact with the Republic, Oon Tien became a vital part of the Kathol [[Sector/Legends|sector]], with manufacturing and [[Farm/Legends|agriculture]] being important parts of its industry.<ref name="tdc" /> Jarells settled on other worlds in the Kathol sector, including [[Kolatill]],<ref name="o">{{DarkStryder|adventure=Omens|book=The DarkStryder Campaign}}</ref> [[Pembric II]],<ref name="c">{{DarkStryder|adventure=Crisis (adventure)|book=The DarkStryder Campaign}}</ref> and [[Corjain]].<ref name="tdc" />

==Jarells in the galaxy==
Jarells were found in the Kathol sector, usually engaged in their period of indentured servitude to whomever had contracted their services. These jobs took advantage of the Jarells' strength and historically violent nature.<ref name="tdc" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Jarell were created for ''[[The DarkStryder Campaign]]'', a supplement for ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' by [[West End Games]] published in [[1995]].<ref name="tdc" />
